title: vanda aurantiaca subsp. philippinensis
intro: Vanda aurantiaca subsp. philippinensis, also known as the Philippine Vanda, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. It is a type of epiphytic orchid, commonly grown for its vibrant yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Vanda aurantiaca subsp. philippinensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
